front: Photograph: recreates Manet's A Bar at the Folies-Bergère; shows a camera on a tripod in the center between the male photographer and a female model, all reflected in a mirror

Photograph: recreates Delacroix's The Death of Sardanapalus; shows a destroyed room with insulation visible through a torn red wall, broken furniture, a slashed mattress, and high-heels and clothes strewn on the floor
Photograph: recreates a Hokusai print from Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji; shows men in business suits with paper blown into an airstream, staged as a 'non-accidental accident'
back: A Sudden Gust of Wind (Jeff Wall)
work: A Sudden Gust of Wind (after Hokusai) (1993)

front: Photograph: shows a White man pulling at the corner of his eye to mock an Asian man passing him on the street
Photograph: staged scene of wounded soldiers in a trench; sold for $3.7 million in 2012 (then the third most expensive photograph ever sold); analyzed in Susan Sontag's Regarding the Pain of Others
Artist: Canadian conceptual photographer known for large-format staged 'near documentary' photographs; part of the Vancouver photoconceptualist school
Photograph: a 180-degree panoramic image of workers mending a large panoramic painting
